channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Supreme Court rules Trump administration must 'facilitate’ return of mistakenly deported man
item.description: The court’s emergency ruling instructs the lower judge to clarify the language of her order but said she acted properly in ensuring that Kilmar Abrego Garcia’s case is handled “as it would have been had he not been improperly sent to El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KREX 5

item.title: The Latest: Supreme Court tells Trump administration to facilitate return of mistakenly deported man
item.description: The Supreme Court on Thursday said President Donald Trump’s administration must facilitate the return of Kilmar Abrego Garcia, a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported to El Salvador, rejecting the administration’s emergency appeal.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge dismisses charges in alleged campus vigilante 'Catch a Predator' sting targeting Army soldier
item.description: Five Massachusetts college students accused of attacking a man in a "To Catch a Predator" sting have had their kidnapping and conspiracy charges dropped.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Where things stand for Trump in global tariff battle
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In the aftermath of this week's tariff whiplash, President Donald Trump is deciding exactly what he wants out of trade talks with as many as 75 nations in the coming weeks. Trump is also figuring out next steps with China.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Pressed for evidence against Mahmoud Khalil, government cites its power to deport people for beliefs
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Facing a deadline from an immigration judge to turn over evidence for its attempted deportation of Columbia University activist Mahmoud Khalil, the federal government has instead submitted a brief memo, signed by Secretary of State Marco Rubio, citing the Trump administration’s autho
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ukraine 'coalition of the willing' presses ahead with force planning amid doubts about US support
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — Britain's defense minister on Thursday urged his counterparts from around 30 countries to press ahead with plans to deploy troops to Ukraine to police any future peace agreement with Russia, as questions remained over whether the United States would provide backup.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK and SAM McNEIL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Escaped California prison inmate convicted of murder kills police commander in Mexico: report
item.description: Cesar Hernandez, a prison inmate convicted of murder, allegedly was involved in a shootout with Mexican authorities months after escaping from California authorities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Witness captures video of helicopter breaking apart, crashing into Hudson River
item.description: A witness captured video of the moment a helicopter came crashing down into the Hudson River between Manhattan and New Jersey Thursday, killing six people.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Movie theaters erupt in chaos as part of 'Minecraft' social media trend
item.description: Rowdy crowds of young people have been disrupting screenings of the newly released "A Minecraft Movie" around the country as they take part in the so-called "chicken jockey" trend.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pennsylvania judge convicted of shooting ex-boyfriend in head as he slept
item.description: Judge Sonya McKnight was found guilty of attempted homicide and aggravated assault for the 2024 shooting of her ex-boyfriend in Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gambian torture victims testify in US against a member of former dictator's military
item.description: An ex-military officer in Gambia and others have testified this week against a man on trial in the United States accused of conspiring to torture people accused of opposing the West African nation's longtime dictator.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Slevin, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Company says thousands of gallons of oil have been recovered from a pipeline spill in North Dakota
item.description: BISMARCK, N.D. (AP) — Workers have recovered thousands of gallons of crude oil from an underground pipeline spill on North Dakota farmland, the owner of the line said Thursday, but it remains unclear when oil will again start flowing to refineries.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DeSantis defends $10 million donation from state agency settlement to charity linked to his wife
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis on Thursday defended a state agency's involvement with a $10 million donation to a charity founded by his wife as a “cherry on top” deal using funds from a settlement between the agency and the state’s largest Medicaid contractor.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANY MATAT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US egg prices increase to record high, dashing hopes of cheap eggs by Easter
item.description: U.S. egg prices increased again last month to reach a new record-high of $6.23 per dozen despite President Donald Trump’s predictions, a drop in wholesale prices and no egg farms having bird flu outbreaks.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK and DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wisconsin Democratic leader who revitalized state party in swing state is leaving
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — The Wisconsin Democratic leader who revitalized the party in the presidential swing state announced Thursday he will be leaving his position in June after a six-year run that included a string of victories and “agonizingly close” losses.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump orders agencies to ‘sunset’ environmental protections
item.description: President Trump directed agencies that regulate energy and the environment to sunset a wide array of environmental protections in an executive order issued Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Hip-hop exec, accused LA gang leader pleads not guilty to racketeering, extortion charges
item.description: A music executive who authorities allege is a leader of a notorious Los Angeles street gang appeared in court Tuesday to face a wide range of charges, including extortion, racketeering and conspiracy.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, WGN 9

item.title: Judge will halt Trump administration from ending humanitarian parole for people from four countries
item.description: A federal judge says she will halt the Trump administration from ending a program that allowed Cubans, Haitians, Nicaraguans and Venezuelans to temporarily live in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Meet the Gulf shrimpers rooting on Trump's tariffs in a Texas fishing town
item.description: While American consumers and markets wonder and worry about President Donald Trump’s on-again, off-again tariffs, Gulf coast shrimpers are cheering him on.
item.dc:creator: Lekan Oyekanmi And Jim Vertuno, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Man arrested for 'economic interruption' after dump truck crash causes Draper power outage
item.description: Police arrested a man for allegedly knocking down power poles while driving a dump truck with the lift gate up on Wednesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, ABC 4

item.title: NYPD searches for scooter-riding suspect who left brick with swastika on Tesla Cybertruck in Jewish community
item.description: The NYPD is searching for a scooter-riding woman who left trash and a brick with swastika on a Tesla Cybertruck in a Brooklyn Jewish community.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Annual White House Christmas ornament honors state dinner culinary diplomacy
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The art of culinary diplomacy, practiced by presidents at White House state dinners for more than 150 years, is the theme of this year's official White House Christmas ornament.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mourners pay tribute to merengue icon Rubby Pérez, who died in the Dominican roof collapse
item.description: Fans and countrymen on Thursday shared their remembrances of Dominican Republic music icon Rubby Pérez, who was among the scores of people killed this week after the roof at the Jet Set club in Santo Domingo collapsed. He was 69.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Gun discharges in student's backpack at Maryland elementary school, officials say
item.description: The student had allegedly brought the gun from home but shared that they had no intentions of hurting anyone, according to CCPS.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gable, NewsNation

item.title: Hundreds of students, dozens of colleges hit by Trump's visa purge: What to know
item.description: International students are seeing their visas revoked for infractions as minor as traffic violations.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Johnson scraps vote on Trump budget blueprint in face of conservative opposition
item.description: House Republican leaders on Wednesday scrapped plans to vote on the Senate’s framework to advance key parts of President Trump’s legislative agenda, a major setback that came in the face of opposition from hard-line conservatives. Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Brawl erupts in Kansas courtroom during murder sentencing
item.description: The murder victim's mother spoke to the judge before the sentencing. Her words sparked anger among some of the other people in the courtroom.
item.dc:creator: Carina Branson, NewsNation

item.title: State leaders tell Trump cabinet members that Utah should run its own federal programs
item.description: Utah's Speaker of the House Mike Schultz is among the top state leaders pushing for Utah to have control over federal programs.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Aerts, ABC 4

item.title: Power restored after dump truck crashes into power poles in Draper
item.description: Traffic lights in Draper are being impacted by a power outage that was caused by a dump truck crash on Wednesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Tips for spring gardening: Utah experts talk best times to prep and plant
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4) -- Gardening can be many things to different people—sometimes a chore, other times a passion. If you're one of those passionate gardeners or someone looking to get started, here are some tips from experts at Millcreek Gardens to help you get your garden started.
item.dc:creator: Nate Larsen, ABC 4

item.title: Previous execution reveals how South Carolina will put 2nd inmate to death by firing squad
item.description: South Carolina is legally required to keep some protocols secret as it prepares to put a man to death by firing squad for the murder of a public safety officer, but the only previous execution by bullets the state carried out last month reveals a few key details.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: She survived being shot in the head as a teen. Now she's sharing her final message
item.description: Deserae Turner — who survived being shot in the head in 2017 and has undergone several medical procedures since — is now in hospice after recent health complications.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Sam's Club plans to open 15 new locations a year, CEO says
item.description: Sam's Club CEO Chris Nicholas says the company plans to open 15 new locations a year "for the foreseeable future.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, KRON4

item.title: 4 charged after Virginia shooting that killed 3, authorities say
item.description: Four people were charged Wednesday in connection with a shooting that killed three people and wounded three others in Virginia, authorities said. Three suspects have been arrested on charges of malicious wounding and felony firearm use.
item.dc:creator: OLIVIA DIAZ, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump limits tariffs on most nations for 90 days, except China
item.description: Facing a global market meltdown, President Donald Trump on Wednesday abruptly backed down on his tariffs on most nations for 90 days, but raised his tax rate on Chinese imports to 125%.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: YouTuber and entrepreneur MrBeast slams Trump's 'brutal' tariffs
item.description: MrBeast, whose real name is Jimmy Donaldson, called the tariffs a "nail in the coffin" for small businesses.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WGN 9

item.title: Supreme Court clears way for Trump admin to fire some probationary workers
item.description: The Supreme Court sided with the Trump administration Tuesday by enabling officials to fire thousands of federal workers in their probationary period, saying the government employee unions that sued don’t have legal standing.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5
